findings.title: Incoming webhook payloads returned unredacted to admin UI
findings.description: AdminWebhooksController.incomingWebhooks() returns the full 'payload' field from incoming webhooks. Payloads from Stripe/GitHub may contain API keys, webhook secrets, or PII that should not be displayed even to admins.
findings.recommendation: Apply payload sanitization similar to AdminFailedJobsController.sanitizePayload() before returning incoming webhook payloads to the frontend. Redact known sensitive keys (api_key, secret, token, authorization).

findings.title: Webhook delivery response_body returned unredacted
findings.description: AdminWebhooksController.showDelivery() returns the raw response_body from webhook deliveries. Third-party API responses may contain secrets, tokens, or internal system details.
findings.recommendation: Sanitize response_body through the same redaction pipeline as failed job exceptions. At minimum, redact Bearer tokens, API keys, and known secret patterns.

findings.title: No impersonation session timeout independent of global session TTL
findings.description: Impersonation sessions persist for the entire Laravel session lifetime (default 120 minutes). There is no separate, shorter timeout for impersonation. A forgotten impersonation session allows prolonged access to a user's account.
findings.recommendation: Store an 'impersonation_started_at' timestamp in the session. Add middleware or a check in HandleInertiaRequests that auto-terminates impersonation after a configurable timeout (e.g., 15-30 minutes).

findings.title: No MFA/re-authentication gate before impersonation
findings.description: Super-admin impersonation is guarded only by session-based auth. If an admin session is hijacked, the attacker gains impersonation capability immediately. No re-authentication or 2FA confirmation is required.
findings.recommendation: Require password re-entry or 2FA TOTP confirmation before starting impersonation. This is a standard security pattern for high-privilege operations.

findings.title: AdminToggleAdminRequest and AdminToggleActiveRequest have empty rules arrays
findings.description: These Form Requests authorize via isAdmin() but have empty rules() methods. The actual privilege checks (super_admin) are enforced via route middleware, but the form request authorize() only checks isAdmin(), creating a mismatch. If the route middleware were accidentally removed, a regular admin could call these endpoints.
findings.recommendation: Add super_admin check directly in the authorize() method of both Form Requests as defense-in-depth: return $this->user()?->isSuperAdmin() === true. This prevents privilege escalation if middleware is misconfigured.

findings.title: Session management only works with database driver
findings.description: AdminSessionsController only functions when session driver is 'database'. With file/redis/cookie drivers, the index returns an empty list and destroy does nothing. No warning is logged if an admin tries to terminate sessions with an incompatible driver.
findings.recommendation: Return a clear error/warning when session driver is not 'database' instead of silently returning empty results. Consider supporting Redis session enumeration as well.

findings.title: Admin notification broadcast has insufficient rate limiting
findings.description: AdminNotificationsController.send() is throttled at 10/minute but sends notifications to ALL users (or all admins). A compromised admin account could spam the entire user base with 10 broadcasts per minute, each reaching potentially thousands of users.
findings.recommendation: Reduce the broadcast rate limit to 2-3 per minute. Add a cooldown period (e.g., 5 minutes between broadcasts). Consider adding a confirmation step with recipient count preview.

findings.title: No minimum admin count enforcement on bulk deactivate
findings.description: AdminUsersController.bulkDeactivate() excludes the requesting admin but does not check if deactivating selected users would leave fewer than 2 admins. toggleAdmin has this check but bulk deactivate does not.
findings.recommendation: Before executing bulk deactivation, check that the remaining active admin count after deactivation is >= 2. The toggleAdmin method already implements this pattern.

findings.title: AdminConfigController may expose sensitive environment details
findings.description: AdminConfigController displays environment name, timezone, and configuration warnings. While it currently filters to safe data, any future additions to this controller could accidentally expose sensitive config values. There is no explicit allowlist pattern.
findings.recommendation: Implement an explicit allowlist of config keys that can be displayed. Add a test that asserts sensitive keys (database.*, mail.*, services.*) are never returned.

findings.title: Cache flush operation has no confirmation or undo
findings.description: AdminCacheController.flush() can flush all admin caches or category-specific caches. Flushing all caches causes a thundering herd of cache misses hitting the database. No confirmation beyond super_admin auth is required.
findings.recommendation: Add a staggered flush option that invalidates cache keys with randomized TTL offsets. Log the full cache flush with a warning-level audit event.

findings.title: Audit log export metadata is intentionally unfiltered for compliance
findings.description: AdminAuditLogController.export() exports full metadata without redaction, documented as a compliance requirement. However, this means any sensitive data stored in audit log metadata (passwords accidentally logged, tokens, etc.) will be exported in CSV format.
findings.recommendation: While compliance requires full export, add a pre-export scan that warns if metadata contains known sensitive patterns (password, token, secret). Log all exports with the admin's identity.

findings.title: Route-dependent feature flags cannot be toggled at runtime
findings.description: Flags marked as route_dependent (billing, social_auth, api_tokens, api_docs, admin) are registered at boot time. Toggling them in the admin UI changes the resolved value but routes are NOT re-registered, leading to 404s or phantom routes. The UI shows a warning but allows the toggle.
findings.recommendation: Disable the toggle switch in the admin UI for route-dependent flags entirely, or add a prominent banner stating 'Requires application restart to take effect'. The current warning may be insufficient.

findings.title: AdminFeatureFlagController.searchUsers() has minimal input validation
findings.description: The search endpoint validates minimum 2 characters and maximum 100 characters, but does not sanitize the input beyond SQL LIKE escaping. The endpoint is exposed to any admin (not super_admin only).
findings.recommendation: Add rate limiting specific to the search endpoint. Consider restricting to alphanumeric + common email characters to prevent edge cases.

findings.title: Regular admin can view all user details including subscription data
findings.description: AdminUsersController.show() returns subscription details, API token counts, and full audit logs for any user. Regular admins and super_admins see identical data. There is no field-level permission differentiation.
findings.recommendation: Consider restricting subscription detail and billing information to super_admin only, or implement field-level permissions. At minimum, audit when non-super admins access user detail pages.

findings.title: No rate limiting enforcement tests for admin routes
findings.description: Only 1 test verifies that a route has throttle middleware applied. No tests verify that rate limits are actually enforced (e.g., 11th request to a 10/min endpoint returns 429).
findings.recommendation: Add rate limit enforcement tests for critical endpoints: impersonation (5/min), exports (10/min), password reset (5/min), notifications send (10/min). Use Laravel's withoutExceptionHandling() pattern.

findings.title: No tests for webhook payload/response sanitization
findings.description: AdminWebhooksController exposes incoming webhook payloads and delivery response bodies. No tests verify that sensitive data in these fields is redacted.
findings.recommendation: Add tests that create incoming webhooks with sensitive payloads (containing API keys, tokens) and verify the admin endpoint redacts them.

findings.title: Dashboard stats cached for 1 hour but signup chart data may be stale
findings.description: AdminDashboardController caches stats with 1-hour TTL. New user registrations within this window won't appear in dashboard counts. The cache invalidation on registration only invalidates DASHBOARD_STATS and DASHBOARD_SIGNUP_CHART keys, but the controller uses a separate cache key.
findings.recommendation: Verify that CacheInvalidationManager.invalidateOnRegistration() invalidates the same cache keys used by AdminDashboardController. Consider reducing TTL to 5 minutes for real-time accuracy.

findings.title: AdminSystemController exposes PHP version and package versions
findings.description: AdminSystemController returns exact PHP version, Laravel version, Node version, OS details, database version, and installed package versions. This information is valuable for attackers targeting known CVEs. Although restricted to super_admin, a compromised super_admin session exposes the full attack surface.
findings.recommendation: Consider showing only major.minor versions instead of full version strings. Add an audit log when this page is accessed. Consider whether this information truly needs to be in the UI vs. CLI-only.

findings.title: CSV export row limit of 10,000 may be insufficient for large datasets
findings.description: config/pagination.php sets export limit to 10,000 rows. For user exports or audit log exports in production, this may silently truncate data without warning the admin.
findings.recommendation: When the export reaches the row limit, include a final row in the CSV indicating truncation, or add a response header warning of incomplete data. Consider async export with download link for large datasets.

findings.title: Admin user creation allows setting is_admin without super_admin enforcement in Form Request
findings.description: AdminCreateUserRequest conditionally includes is_admin validation rules when the requesting user is super_admin. However, the authorize() method only checks isAdmin(). If validation rules are bypassed or the conditional is removed, any admin could create admin users.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit controller-level check: if request has is_admin=true and user is not super_admin, abort(403). Defense-in-depth beyond the Form Request conditional.

findings.title: Impersonation banner relies on frontend rendering only
findings.description: The impersonation state is communicated to the frontend via Inertia shared props. If a custom API client or non-Inertia request is made during impersonation, there is no server-side indication that the request is impersonated (beyond session data).
findings.recommendation: Add an X-Impersonated-By header to all responses during impersonation for API consumers and logging. Include impersonation context in audit logs for all actions taken during impersonation.

findings.title: Feature flag 'admin' is protected but only checked in FeatureFlagService
findings.description: The PROTECTED_FLAGS constant prevents overriding the 'admin' flag via the feature flag UI. However, direct database manipulation of feature_flag_overrides table could still override it since there's no database-level constraint.
findings.recommendation: Add a database trigger or model event that prevents inserting/updating feature_flag_overrides rows where flag='admin'. Or add a boot-time validation in the model.

findings.title: Failed job retry executes Artisan command with user-supplied UUID
findings.description: AdminFailedJobsController.retry() passes the job ID directly to Artisan::call('queue:retry', ['id' => [$id]]). While the ID comes from the URL parameter (not form data), insufficient validation could allow injection if the parameter constraint is weak.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit UUID format validation on the {id} route parameter. Use a regex constraint: ->where('id', '[a-f0-9-]{36}'). Verify the ID exists in failed_jobs before calling Artisan.

findings.title: Admin keyboard shortcuts limited to search and pagination
findings.description: useAdminKeyboardShortcuts only supports '/' (search), 'n' (next page), 'p' (prev page). Common admin actions like bulk select all, toggle filters, or navigate to specific sections lack keyboard shortcuts.
findings.recommendation: Add keyboard shortcuts for: Ctrl+A (select all), Escape (clear filters), Ctrl+E (export), Ctrl+Shift+F (toggle filter panel). Document shortcuts in a help dialog accessible via '?'.

findings.title: Admin area does not enforce Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors
findings.description: While X-Frame-Options: DENY is set globally, the CSP does not include frame-ancestors directive. Some browsers prefer CSP over X-Frame-Options. The admin panel should explicitly prevent framing via CSP.
findings.recommendation: Add frame-ancestors 'none' to the CSP header for admin routes to provide defense-in-depth against clickjacking.

findings.title: Admin config and system info pages do not log access
findings.description: AdminConfigController and AdminSystemController do not log when these sensitive information pages are viewed. An attacker with admin access could gather reconnaissance without leaving traces.
findings.recommendation: Add AuditService::log() calls for admin.config_viewed and admin.system_info_viewed events to track access to these reconnaissance-valuable pages.

findings.title: AdminCacheKey.flushAll() catches QueryException silently
findings.description: The flushAll() method catches QueryException when querying user IDs for feature flag cache invalidation. On fresh installs this is expected, but in production it could mask legitimate database failures during cache flush.
findings.recommendation: Log the caught exception at warning level instead of silently swallowing it. Only catch the specific 'table not found' exception type.

findings.title: Admin user export includes email addresses in CSV
findings.description: User CSV exports contain email addresses. Depending on jurisdiction (GDPR, CCPA), exporting PII requires logging and justification. The export is logged but there is no consent or purpose tracking.
findings.recommendation: Add a purpose/reason field to export requests. Log the export with the filter parameters and purpose. Consider adding a data processing agreement acceptance step.

findings.title: No tests for admin 2FA enforcement on admin accounts
findings.description: There are no tests verifying whether admin accounts are required to have 2FA enabled. The system allows admins without 2FA, reducing security posture.
findings.recommendation: Consider enforcing 2FA for all admin accounts. Add tests that verify admin routes require 2FA when two_factor feature is enabled.

findings.title: Admin search endpoints share base rate limit
findings.description: User search in feature flags (searchUsers) and admin user index search use the base 60/min admin rate limit. Automated tools could enumerate users at 60 requests/minute.
findings.recommendation: Add specific rate limiting for search endpoints (e.g., 30/min) to prevent rapid user enumeration. Consider adding response delay for failed searches.

findings.title: Roadmap reorder accepts arbitrary display_order values
findings.description: AdminReorderRoadmapRequest validates items as an array with id, status, and display_order (integer) but does not validate that display_order values are sequential or bounded.
findings.recommendation: Add min:0 and max constraints to display_order. Consider normalizing order values server-side regardless of client input.

findings.title: Admin area uses same session cookie as regular application
findings.description: Admin sessions use the same Laravel session cookie as regular user sessions. Session fixation after impersonation or admin escalation affects both contexts.
findings.recommendation: Consider using a separate session cookie name or prefix for admin routes, or ensure session regeneration occurs on every admin privilege transition.

findings.title: Health check results cached for 5 seconds without configuration
findings.description: HealthCheckService caches results using config('health.cache_ttl', 5) which defaults to 5 seconds. While configurable, monitoring systems polling at <5s intervals will receive stale data.
findings.recommendation: Document the cache TTL behavior for monitoring integrations. Consider adding a ?nocache query parameter for urgent health checks (with separate rate limiting).

findings.title: AdminBulkFeedbackRequest allows 'delete' action for regular admins
findings.description: The bulk feedback request validates action as in:delete,resolve,decline. However, individual feedback deletion requires super_admin via route middleware. The bulk endpoint may not enforce the same restriction for the 'delete' action.
findings.recommendation: Verify that the bulkUpdate controller method checks for super_admin when action is 'delete'. If not, add the check to prevent regular admins from bulk-deleting feedback.

findings.title: Social auth admin page has minimal test coverage
findings.description: AdminSocialAuthTest.php has only 3 tests covering the dashboard stats display. No tests for edge cases like providers with expired tokens or disconnected accounts.
findings.recommendation: Add tests for: zero social accounts, multiple providers, expired tokens display, provider-specific stats accuracy.

findings.title: Admin panel uses 25 controllers — consider grouping into sub-namespaces
findings.description: All 25 admin controllers are in a flat Admin/ directory. As the panel grows, this could become harder to navigate. Related controllers (billing, webhooks, system) could benefit from sub-namespaces.
findings.recommendation: Consider grouping into Admin/Billing/, Admin/System/, Admin/Content/ sub-namespaces for maintainability. Not urgent but helpful for navigation.

findings.title: 454+ admin test cases provide excellent coverage
findings.description: The admin panel has 46 test files with 454+ test cases covering authentication, authorization, CRUD operations, edge cases, cache invalidation, and security. This is well above industry average.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current test discipline. Focus new test efforts on the gaps identified in this audit (webhook payload sanitization, rate limiting enforcement, impersonation timeout).

findings.title: Dual-role system (admin/super_admin) is well-implemented
findings.description: The admin/super_admin separation with dedicated middleware, route-level enforcement, and 20+ super_admin-only operations provides strong privilege separation. Mass assignment protection on is_admin and super_admin fields is correctly implemented.
findings.recommendation: No changes needed. The current implementation correctly prevents privilege escalation through mass assignment, form requests, and middleware layers.

findings.title: CSV formula injection prevention is correctly implemented
findings.description: CsvExport.sanitize() properly prefixes dangerous characters (=, +, -, @, tab, CR) with single quotes. UTF-8 BOM is included for Excel compatibility. All admin export endpoints use this sanitization.
findings.recommendation: No changes needed. Current implementation follows OWASP CSV injection prevention guidelines.

findings.title: Comprehensive audit trail with IP anonymization for GDPR compliance
findings.description: AuditService logs all admin operations with IP anonymization for non-security events (IPv4 last octet zeroed, IPv6 last 80 bits zeroed). Security events retain full IP. 90-day configurable retention.
findings.recommendation: No changes needed. Consider adding a data retention automation (scheduled command) to enforce the 90-day retention policy automatically.

findings.title: Failed job payload sanitization is thorough
findings.description: AdminFailedJobsController sanitizes both payloads (removes serialized command data) and exceptions (redacts passwords, tokens, secrets, API keys, DSN passwords, Stripe keys) using 4 regex patterns plus recursive key redaction.
findings.recommendation: No changes needed. This is a strong implementation. Consider extracting sanitization logic into a reusable service for webhook payloads (see ADM-001).

findings.title: Admin navigation is feature-flag gated with proper conditional rendering
findings.description: getVisibleAdminGroups() filters navigation items based on enabled feature flags. Feature-gated sections (billing, webhooks, tokens, social auth, notifications, 2FA) only appear when their respective features are enabled.
findings.recommendation: No changes needed. Server-side route gating complements frontend navigation filtering for defense-in-depth.

priority_remediation_order.title: Critical data exposure fixes
priority_remediation_order.description: Sanitize incoming webhook payloads and delivery response bodies before returning to admin UI. Extract sanitization from AdminFailedJobsController into a reusable SensitiveDataRedactor service.

priority_remediation_order.title: Impersonation hardening
priority_remediation_order.description: Add impersonation session timeout (15-30 min), require re-authentication before impersonation, add X-Impersonated-By header to all responses during impersonation.

priority_remediation_order.title: Authorization defense-in-depth
priority_remediation_order.description: Add super_admin checks directly in Form Request authorize() methods. Add minimum admin count enforcement to bulk deactivate. Verify bulk feedback delete requires super_admin.

priority_remediation_order.title: Test coverage gaps
priority_remediation_order.description: Add rate limiting enforcement tests, webhook payload sanitization tests, and admin 2FA enforcement tests.

priority_remediation_order.title: Remaining medium/low findings
priority_remediation_order.description: Address remaining medium and low severity findings including session driver warnings, notification rate limiting, config allowlisting, and cache management improvements.

strengths: Comprehensive dual-role (admin/super_admin) authorization with middleware and form request layers
454+ admin test cases across 46 test files with excellent coverage
CSV formula injection prevention correctly implemented on all export endpoints
Failed job payload and exception sanitization is thorough with 4 regex patterns
IP anonymization for GDPR compliance with security event exemption
Feature-flag-gated navigation and routes with server-side enforcement
Cache invalidation manager provides centralized, domain-aware cache management
Audit logging on all state-changing admin operations with metadata
Mass assignment protection prevents privilege escalation on admin fields
Session regeneration on impersonation start/stop prevents fixation
Rate limiting on all admin mutation endpoints with appropriate per-route limits
Encrypted impersonation session data prevents tampering
